content.rendered:
What’s more unlikely than Bruce Springsteen covering “You Sexy Thing,” the surprise 1975 B-side smash hit (it ranked #22 for the entire year) by Hot Chocolate?

How about turning it into a steamy duet with a fellow Bruce?

On the evenings of December 7 and 8 during Bruce’s 2001 holiday show stand in Asbury Park, one of the far-and-away highlights of those loose, festive shows was the “You Sexy Thing” duet between Bruces Springsteen and Hornsby. And if their vocal performances weren’t hot enough, add Hornsby’s star turn on the accordion and a spot-on sax solo from The Big Man, and you’ve got the recipe for a showstopper.

Take a listen to one of my favorite under-the-radar covers:

Bruce teased fans with “You Sexy Thing” snippets more than a dozen times in the decade that followed, but these two special nights remain the only two times Bruce has ever played it in full.

You Sexy Thing
First performed: December 7, 2001 (Asbury Park, NJ)
Last performed: December 8, 2001 (Asbury  Park, NJ)

© September 22, 2019

excerpt.rendered: It doesn’t get steamier than two Bruces teaming up for an accordion-powered duet on Hot Chocolate’s 1975 surprise hit, “You Sexy Thing.”

jetpack-related-posts.title: Sexy and I Know It
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: A self-spoofing Bruce joins Jimmy Fallon-as-Neil Young for a hilarious take on LMFAO's "Sexy and I Know It."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Whip My Hair
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce and Jimmy Fallon (as Neil Young) perform the unlikeliest of covers in one of Bruce's most viral moments. Watch the performance and backstory inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Let’s Go, Let’s Go, Let’s Go
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Days after Hank Ballard's passing, Bruces Springsteen and Hornsby (along with former Steel Mill bandmate Robbin Thompson) pay tribute by playing Ballard's last great hit.

jetpack-related-posts.title: MatR: Dave Edmunds and Bruce Springsteen, Let’s Talk About Us
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce teams up with Dave Edmunds at Big Man's West on Jerry Lee Lewis' "Let's Talk About Us."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Lean on Me
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce and Nils celebrate their friendship in a 1989 performance of Bill Withers' greatest hit.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Come Together
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Axl Rose's last public appearance for six years was an almost literal last-minute duet with Bruce Springsteen in 1994 at the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame.
